label: Ebla (Tell Mardikh)
sub: Bronze Age city-state (c. 3000–2300 BCE). Palace archive of 17,000 cuneiform tablets. Excavated by Italian University of Rome La Sapienza since 1964.

label: Mari (Tell Hariri)
sub: Major Euphrates kingdom (c. 2900–1759 BCE). Palace of Zimri-Lim and 20,000 cuneiform tablets. Destroyed by Hammurabi of Babylon. Near Abu Kamal.

label: Palmyra (Tadmur)
sub: UNESCO World Heritage Site. Roman-era desert caravan city; capital of Queen Zenobia's empire (270–273 CE). ISIS destroyed significant monuments in 2015. Reconstruction underway.

label: Ugarit (Ras Shamra)
sub: Bronze Age port city (c. 2000–1200 BCE) near Latakia. Birthplace of one of the earliest alphabets (Ugaritic script). Excavated by French since 1929.

label: Dura-Europos
sub: Hellenistic-Roman garrison town (c. 300 BCE–256 CE) on the Euphrates. Contains earliest known Christian house church and well-preserved synagogue murals. Looted during Syrian civil war.

label: Krak des Chevaliers
sub: UNESCO World Heritage Site. 12th-century Crusader castle held by Knights Hospitaller. Captured by Mamluk Sultan Baybars in 1271. Heavily damaged in 2012–2014 Syrian civil war fighting.

label: Apamea (Afamia)
sub: Seleucid / Roman city (c. 300 BCE–7th CE). Famous for its 2-km colonnaded street. One of the largest cities of the ancient Roman Near East. Severely looted by mechanical diggers during the civil war.

label: Golan Heights (Occupied)
sub: Occupied by Israel since June 1967 Six-Day War; formally annexed 1981. US recognized Israeli sovereignty in 2019. ~20,000 Israeli settlers; ~22,000 Syrian Druze residents. Status unresolved under international law.

label: Kobani (Ayn al-Arab)
sub: Kurdish-majority town besieged by ISIS October 2014–January 2015. US-backed YPG/SDF, with US airstrikes, defeated ISIS in 4-month battle. Kobani became symbol of Kurdish resistance and led to SDF-US partnership.

label: Raqqa
sub: Former ISIS caliphate capital (2014–2017). Liberated by US-backed SDF in October 2017 after 4-month battle; city 80% destroyed. Under SDF/AANES administration since. ~250,000 pre-war population.

label: Deir ez-Zor
sub: Oil-rich Euphrates valley city. Assad besieged 2013–2017 while ISIS surrounded it. US-backed SDF and Assad forces raced to control oil fields after ISIS defeat (2017). Ongoing low-level ISIS insurgency and SDF-Damascus tensions.

label: Daraa
sub: Birthplace of the 2011 Syrian uprising. Security forces shot protesters in March 2011 after arresting teenagers for anti-Assad graffiti. Besieged 2018; 'reconciled' under Assad. Under transitional government since Dec 2024.

label: Douma (Eastern Ghouta)
sub: Site of April 7, 2018 chlorine gas attack killing ~43 civilians. OPCW confirmed chlorine use; attribution contested. Earlier siege of Eastern Ghouta (Feb–March 2018) killed ~1,700. Area retaken by Assad forces April 2018.

label: Baghouz
sub: Site of final battle against ISIS territorial caliphate (February–March 2019). SDF/US forces captured last village held by ISIS on March 23, 2019. Tens of thousands of ISIS families surrendered. Near Syrian-Iraqi border.

label: Aleppo
sub: Syria's second city. Major battle 2012–2016 (Assad retook Dec 2016). Fell to HTS rebel forces Nov 29–30, 2024 in hours. Currently under transitional government. ~2 million pre-war residents; significant war damage.

label: Yarmouk Refugee Camp
sub: Former unofficial Palestinian refugee camp in Damascus, housing 160,000 at peak. Besieged by Assad forces 2012–2018; partially occupied by ISIS in 2015. Reduced to rubble by war. ~10,000 Palestinians returned after Assad's fall seeking homes.

label: Al-Hol Camp
sub: Largest detention camp for ISIS-affiliated families in NE Syria. Holds ~65,000 people (as of 2025) — mostly women and children from ISIS-held areas, including ~10,000 foreigners. Administered by SDF. Acute security and humanitarian crisis.

label: Idlib Displacement Hub
sub: Northwest Syria concentrated ~4 million IDPs before Dec 2024 — the world's largest IDP concentration. HTS-administered since 2019. Major humanitarian corridor for USAID/UN aid. Situation improving post-regime collapse.

label: Za'atari Refugee Camp (Jordan)
sub: World's largest Syrian refugee camp in northern Jordan. Houses ~80,000 refugees (peak 156,000 in 2013). Established August 2012. Managed by UNHCR/Jordan. ~660,000 Syrian refugees remain in Jordan total.

label: Damascus (Transitional Capital)
sub: Syrian capital since ancient times; Umayyad Caliphate capital 661–750 CE. Assad regime fell Dec 8, 2024. Now seat of transitional government under Ahmad al-Sharaa. Population ~2.5 million.

label: Aleppo Administration
sub: Second-largest Syrian city. Transitional government administrative council established after rebel takeover (Nov 2024). Key economic centre; reconstruction of war damage a priority. UNESCO World Heritage old city.

label: Qamishli (SDF/AANES)
sub: De facto capital of the Autonomous Administration of North and East Syria (AANES/Rojava). SDF military headquarters. Home to significant Kurdish, Arab, and Assyrian Christian populations. Ongoing negotiations with transitional government.

label: Al-Hasakah (SDF Admin)
sub: Main city of Syria's northeast; SDF/AANES administrative hub. Contains significant oil infrastructure. Multi-ethnic (Arab, Kurdish, Assyrian, Armenian). Transitional government and SDF negotiating control.

label: Turkish-Controlled Northern Strip
sub: Turkey and its Syrian National Army (SNA) proxies control ~4,000 sq km of northern Syria including Afrin, Ras al-Ayn, Tal Abyad, and Azaz. Turkey-backed governance structures in place. Transitional government and Turkey in complex negotiations.

label: Sweida (Druze Heartland)
sub: Druze-majority southern province. Largely autonomous during Assad era (Druze refused conscription). Massive anti-Assad protests erupted August 2023. Under transitional government oversight since 2024; Druze community negotiating special status.

label: Bab al-Hawa Crossing
sub: Syria–Turkey border crossing in Idlib. Main UN-authorized humanitarian aid corridor into northwest Syria since 2014. HTS-controlled; handles ~1,000 trucks/month of aid. UN Security Council authorization for cross-border aid repeatedly contested by Russia.

label: Bab al-Salameh Crossing
sub: Syria–Turkey border crossing near Azaz in Aleppo province. Turkish-SNA controlled. Key entry point for Turkish-backed humanitarian aid and commercial traffic to northern Syria. ~200,000 IDPs in adjacent camps (post-2019).

label: Nassib (Jaber) Crossing
sub: Syria–Jordan border crossing. Closed by rebel takeover 2015–2018; reopened July 2018 under Russian pressure. Key commercial route; ~$1B annual trade pre-war. Reopened fully after Assad's fall in Dec 2024.

label: Abu Kamal Border Crossing
sub: Syria–Iraq border crossing on the Euphrates. Key strategic node on the 'land corridor' Iran used to supply Hezbollah via Iraq–Syria. SDF and transitional government compete for control; Iranian influence sharply reduced after Assad's fall.

label: Homs
sub: Syria's third-largest city; 'capital of the revolution' (2011–2014). Old city besieged 2012–2014; massive destruction. Assad retook Homs June 2014. Fell to HTS/rebel forces December 7, 2024. Under transitional government.
